# Gajar Ka Halwa (Carrot Halwa)
ByDassana Amit UpdatedJun 25, 2025
30.9K shares
  * [ ](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&t=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20%28Carrot%20Halwa%29 "Share on Facebook")
  * [ ](https://wa.me/?text=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F+Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20%28Carrot%20Halwa%29 "Share on WhatsApp")
  * [ ](https://x.com/intent/tweet?text=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20%28Carrot%20Halwa%29&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&via=vegrecipeindia "Share on X")
  * [ ](https://www.reddit.com/submit?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&title=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20%28Carrot%20Halwa%29 "Share on Reddit")
  * 

[](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#wprm-recipe-container-137693)
Gajar Ka Halwa, also called Carrot Halwa or Gajrela in Punjabi, is a classic Indian dessert made by simmering freshly grated carrots in milk, sweetened with sugar, and enriched with ghee. It is especially popular during winters and festivals, loved for its soft, juicy texture. Whether you follow the traditional stovetop method or use shortcuts like [milkmaid](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-carrot-halwa-with-milkmaid), [khoya](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-gajar-ka-halwa-with-khoya), or the [instant pot](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-instant-pot-carrot-halwa), I’ve got you covered. This post includes step-by-step photos and tips for making gajar halwa in four easy ways.
## About Carrot Halwa
The word halwa comes from the Arabic word for “sweet,” and refers to desserts that are cooked with ghee and sugar. 
Whether made with vermicelli ([Seviyan Halwa](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/meethi-seviyan-recipe/)), lentils ([Moong Dal Halwa](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/moong-dal-halwa-recipe-halwa-recipes/)), grains like [Suji Halwa](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/sooji-halwa-recipe-rava-sheera/), or vegetables like carrot or [Beetroot Halwa](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/beetroot-halwa-recipe-halwa-recipes/) or even nuts like [Almond Halwa](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/almond-halwa-badam-halwa-recipe/), Indian halwa is a pudding that is absolutely delectable.
![gajar ka halwa in a bronze bowl on a bronze plate with a bronze spoon by side](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201200%201652'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2021%2F11%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-1.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
On this page, you’ll find not just a traditional Gajar Ka Halwa recipe, but also three other ways to prepare it, making this a complete guide for carrot halwa lovers.
## Table of Contents
  * [About Carrot Halwa](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-about-carrot-halwa)
  * [How To Make Gajar ka Halwa](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-how-to-make-gajar-ka-halwa)
  * [Carrot Halwa With Milkmaid](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-carrot-halwa-with-milkmaid)
  * [Gajar ka Halwa With Khoya](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-gajar-ka-halwa-with-khoya)
  * [Instant Pot Carrot Halwa](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-instant-pot-carrot-halwa)
  * [Dassana’s Recipe Notes](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-expert-tips)
  * [Asked & Answered](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-faqs)


Contrary to popular belief, Indian halwa is different from Middle Eastern halva, which is a candy made primarily from sesame seeds.
Halwa is a yummy dessert that is reserved for celebratory occasions. It isn’t difficult to make, but it does tend to take a bit of time. If you’d like to make gajar ka halwa more of a regular occurrence, consider using one of my genius shortcuts listed below! 
It is a popular sweet made in North India, and it is especially popular during winters. The traditional recipe is made with only carrots, whole (full-fat) milk, ghee and sugar; there’s no need for khoya (evaporated milk solids) or condensed milk.
If you prefer using either [khoya](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-gajar-ka-halwa-with-khoya) or [milkmaid (sweetened condensed milk)](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-carrot-halwa-with-milkmaid), skip to the appropriate section below to learn how!
I prefer to use red Delhi carrots to make the halwa, as they are tender and juicy. That said, you can use any carrots – red, orange, or even black – so long as they are not fibrous, stringy, or hard.
I still remember winter evenings when the house filled with the cozy aroma of grated carrots bubbling in creamy, simmering milk. My mother stirred patiently, never rushing the simmer; that slow magic is what I have captured in this recipe.
One of the reasons that I love making this halwa is that it has a pretty long shelf life. I can make a batch that’ll last for upwards of 10 days in the fridge, meaning we always have something sweet to snack on after our meals.
## How To Make Gajar ka Halwa
### Prep & Grate Carrots
1. First rinse 650 grams carrots (8 to 9 medium-sized tender, juicy carrots or 6 to 7 long carrots) a few times in water. 
Drain the water. Peel the carrots using a vegetable peeler. Set the peeled carrots aside.
Ensure that the carrots are juicy and tender. You could use any variety of carrots (red or orange), but they should be tender.
![peeled red delhi carrots on a cutting board.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa01.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
2. Next, grate the carrots with a box grater or in a food processor. You can measure the grated carrots with a standard measuring cup and you will need about 4 to 4.5 cups of grated carrots.
Grating carrots in a food processor is much easier than grating with a box grater or a hand-held grater.
![grated delhi red carrots to make gajar ka halwa.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa02.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
### Cook Carrots
3. In a heavy kadai or wok, add all of the grated carrots.
![delhi red carrots added to kadai.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa03.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
4. Pour in 4 cups full-fat milk. Switch on the burner.
![milk added to kadai with carrots.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa04.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
5. Mix the grated carrots and milk together.
![stirring carrots and milk as first step of making carrot halwa.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa05.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
6. Cook this mixture on a low-medium heat. After a few minutes, the milk will begin to froth and then start slowly reducing.
![simmering gajar ka halwa milk and carrot mixture.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa06.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
7. Keep stirring this halwa mixture often, scraping the sides of the kadai to remove the evaporated milk solids. Stir the milk solids into the cooking mixture. 
![reducing milk in carrot halwa mixture.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa07.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
8. Cook the carrot and milk mixture until the liquid is reduced by 75%.
![continuing to reduce milk in gajar ka halwa.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa08.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
### Make Gajar Halwa
9. Add 4 tablespoons ghee (clarified butter). Note that some milk will be there when you add the ghee.
![ghee added to pan with carrot halwa mix.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa09.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
10. Mix very well.
![carrot milk mix after adding ghee.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa10.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
11. Next, add 10 to 12 tablespoons sugar, or to taste.
![sugar added to gajar ka halwa.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa11.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
12. Add cardamom powder – 5 to 6 green cardamom pods powdered finely in a mortar-pestle, or about ½ to 1 teaspoon pre-ground cardamom powder. 
![cardamom powder added.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa12.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
13. Mix very well.
![carrot halwa after adding sugar and cardamom.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa13.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
14. Continue to simmer on a low flame, stirring often.
![simmering carrot halwa after sugar and spice is added.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa14.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
15. Simmer till the mixture starts to thicken and reduces more. Stir at intervals.
![carrot mixture is becoming thick and the milk is evaporating.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa15.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
16. When the halwa has reduced to a pudding-like consistency, add nuts and dry fruits of your choice. I have added 12 cashews, 12 almonds, and 2 tablespoons golden raisins.   
  
_Note: Chop the nuts before you add. Raisins can be kept whole. If using saffron, then you can add at this step._
![chopped nuts and raisins added to gajar ka halwa.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa16.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
17. Mix again and continue to stir and simmer.
![simmering thickened carrot halwa.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa17.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
18. Simmer till the halwa mixture becomes dry. The milk should evaporate completely and you will see fine milk solids in the mixture. You will also see some ghee being released from the sides. 
Remember to scrape the milk solids stuck at the sides of the kadai or pan and add them back to the halwa. Some moisture is also fine in the halwa.
![delhi red carrot halwa after reducing down to a thick, almost fudgy consistency.](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20750%20562'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F02%2Fcarrot-halwa19.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
19. Serve gajar ka halwa warm from the pan. Refrigerate any leftovers, warming them before serving. Garnish with some chopped dry fruits while serving.
![overhead shot of delhi red carrot halwa filled in a bronze bowl placed on a bronze plate](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201200%20987'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2020%2F12%2Fcarrot-halwa-1.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
## Carrot Halwa With Milkmaid
This is a quick and easy version of carrot halwa made with milkmaid or sweetened condensed milk. I am adding this recipe as a few readers did ask me on how to make this halwa with milkmaid.
Winter is the time of long, tender and juicy carrots in India. So when I get them, I often end up making halwa with them.
I usually make the halwa with the traditional method of cooking the grated carrots in milk (the above method). But on occasions I also take a shortcut of making gajar halwa with condensed milk. This is a quick method than the traditional one and takes less time.
I did use 1.5 kgs carrots and it took about 45 mins to make the halwa. So if you halve or reduce the proportion, it will be much quicker. I used sweetened condensed milk (Nestle Milkmaid) and did not have to add any sugar.
### You Will Need
  * 1.5 kg carrots – approximate 9 cups tightly packed grated carrots
  * 6 tablespoon ghee (clarified butter)
  * 1.5 to 2 teaspoons cardamom powder or 8 to 9 green cardamoms – powdered in a mortar-pestle
  * 1 tin sweetened condensed milk – I used nestle milkmaid, 400 grams
  * sugar as required, optional
  * ⅓ cup cashews
  * ⅓ cup golden raisins


### How to Make
To make the halwa with milkmaid or condensed milk, follow these steps:
1. Melt 6 tablespoons ghee in a heavy kadai or thick bottomed skillet or pan. Add in 9 cups of tightly packed grated carrots. 
![grated carrots in a heavy kadai/wok](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20600%20400'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F03%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe1.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
2. Sauté the carrots until they turn tender and are cooked, about 15 to 20 minutes on low heat. Stir often. 
![sautéed carrots in kadai](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20600%20400'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F03%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe2.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
3. Pour in an entire can of milkmaid or sweetened condensed milk (400 grams).
![condensed milk being poured on carrots](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20600%20400'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F03%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe3.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
4. Mix thoroughly.
![carrots and condensed milk mixed well](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20600%20400'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F03%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe4.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
5. Add 1.5 to 2 teaspoons cardamom powder, ⅓ cup raisins and ⅓ cup cashews.
![dry fruits added to carrot halwa mixture](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20600%20400'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F03%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe5.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
6. Mix well and simmer gajar ka halwa on a low heat. Stir often at intervals until the mixture thickens, reduces and dries up. 
![carrot halwa with condensed milk cooked in kadai](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20600%20400'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F03%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe6.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
7. Serve it hot or warm or chilled.
![collage of carrot halwa in a box and gajar halwa squares in a white plate with a spoon by side](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20600%20446'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2014%2F03%2Fgajar-halwa-with-milkmaid.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
## Gajar ka Halwa With Khoya
This variant is a delicious, quick method to make the halwa with khoya or mawa (milk solids), fresh tender carrots, sugar, ghee and nuts.
Khoya (or evaporated milk solids) gives a lovely texture and flavor to this halwa. I have used homemade khoya to make this treat but you can also use store-bought khoya.
Gajar halwa is made quite often at our place during winters. During winters it is a different fun altogether to eat a piping hot bowl of halwa.
### Ingredients
  * 1.5 kilogram carrots or 9 cups tightly packed grated carrots
  * 6 tablespoons [Ghee](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/ghee/) or clarified butter
  * 2 cups khoya or mawa – grated or crumbled (evaporated milk solids) or 400 to 410 grams
  * 1 to 1.25 cups sugar or add as required
  * 1.5 to 2 teaspoons cardamom powder or 8 to 9 green cardamoms – powdered in a mortar-pestle
  * 1 tablespoon pistachios, chopped or slivered – optional
  * ⅓ cup cashews, or add as required
  * ⅓ cup golden raisins or add as required


### Method
1. Heat 6 tablespoons ghee in a deep bottomed heavy pan or kadai. Add 9 cups tightly packed grated carrots.
![grated carrots in pan](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20664%20500'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F11%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe08.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
2. On a low heat, sauté carrots in ghee, stirring often until they turn tender and are cooked – about 15 to 20 minutes. 
![grated carrots being sautéed in ghee](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20664%20500'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F11%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe11.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
3. Add 2 cups of crumbled or grated khoya (a.k.a. mawa or evaporated milk solids). 
![crumbled soft khoya pieces on top of sautéed carrots in pan](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20664%20500'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F11%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe12.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
4. First mix thoroughly. 
![khoya mixed well with carrots](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20664%20500'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F11%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe14.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
5. Then add 1 to 1.25 cups sugar. Adjust the amount of sugar depending upon the sweetness of your khoya. If your khoya is sweet, start with just 1 cup of sugar. 
![mound of sugar on carrot mixture](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20664%20500'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F11%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe15.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
6. Combine and mix well.
![sugar mixed with carrots](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20664%20500'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F11%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe16.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
7. You will see that both the khoya and sugar will melt. 
![sugar has melted and carrot mixture looks liquid](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20664%20500'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F11%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe17.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
8. Now add ⅓ cup of cashews, ⅓ cup golden raisins, 1 tablespoon pistachios (chopped) and 1.5 to 2 teaspoons cardamom powder.
![dry fruits and cardamom powder on carrot halwa mixture in pan](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20664%20500'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F11%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe18.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
9. Mix again and, stirring often, simmer gajar halwa on low heat. The halwa will start thickening, reducing and leave the edges of the pan. When you see these signs, switch off the heat.
![gajar halwa made with khoya thickened and cooked in pan](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20664%20500'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F11%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe20.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
10. Serve gajar ka halwa either warm or chilled. 
![quick gajar ka halwa with khoya filled in a white bowl and garnished with one cashew placed on a paper doily](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20703%20413'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F11%2Fgajar-ka-halwa-recipe-1a.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
## Instant Pot Carrot Halwa
Instant pot gajar halwa! Yes gajar halwa made in the instant pot in about 15 minutes. So obviously a very quick method to make this delicious sweet in the IP.
Earlier I used to make gajar halwa in a stovetop pressure cooker and have now moved on to making it in the instant pot. I have to admit that instant pot is one of my favorite gadgets to cook with. Indian food adapts very well in the instant pot and gives great results.
Traditionally this Indian carrot pudding is slow-cooked and simmered for hours with frequent stirrings. With the instant pot, it just takes 15 minutes to make the halwa with the same taste and texture as the traditionally made one. If you don’t believe me, you have to try it yourself.
Cooking the halwa in the IP requires less handwork and stirrings as compared to the traditional slow-cooked method.
This recipe uses three main ingredients for making the halwa – fresh juicy carrots, milk and sugar. I add some nuts, dry fruits and a bit of cardamom powder for more taste and aroma.
### Ingredients
  * 500 grams carrots or 4 to 4.5 cups grated carrots
  * 1.5 cups full-fat milk or whole milk
  * ½ cup sugar or add as required
  * 4 tablespoons Ghee (clarified butter)
  * 1 tablespoon raisins
  * 1 tablespoon cashews – chopped
  * 1 tablespoon almonds – sliced
  * 1 tablespoon pistachios – sliced
  * ½ teaspoon cardamom powder (ground cardamom) or 4 to 5 green cardamoms, powdered in mortar-pestle


### Instructions
Making gajar halwa in the Instant Pot is super easy as you do not have to spend time stirring it. Follow these easy steps to make the recipe:
1. Add 4 to 4.5 cups grated carrots, 1.5 cups whole milk, ½ cup sugar, and 4 tablespoons ghee in the steel insert of a 6 quart Instant Pot.
![carrot halwa ingredients in instant pot](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20487'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F01%2Finstant-pot-gajar-halwa03.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
2. Mix all of the ingredients with a spoon to make an even mixture.
![carrot halwa mixture in instant pot](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20487'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F01%2Finstant-pot-gajar-halwa04.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
3. Seal with the Instant Pot lid. Position the valve to the sealing position. Pressure cook on high for 3 minutes.
![outer panel of instant pot](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20487'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F01%2Finstant-pot-gajar-halwa05.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
4. After 3 minutes of pressure cooking (it should beep), carefully do a quick pressure release (QPR) by lifting the pressure valve. When all the pressure is released, open the lid. Stir and mix very well. 
![pressure cooked carrot halwa mixture](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20487'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F01%2Finstant-pot-gajar-halwa06.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
5. Press cancel and then press the sauté button, setting the time to 5 to 10 minutes on normal mode.
![outer panel of instant pot](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20487'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F01%2Finstant-pot-gajar-halwa07.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
6. The mixture will begin to simmer and bubble. Continue to stir often until the halwa thickens.
![simmering gajar halwa mixture in instant pot](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20487'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F01%2Finstant-pot-gajar-halwa09.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
7. When the halwa has almost thickened, add 1 tablespoons each of sliced or chopped almonds, cashews, pistachios and raisins. Add ½ teaspoon cardamom powder.
![nuts, raisins and cardamom powder added to simmering carrot halwa mixture in instant pot](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20487'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F01%2Finstant-pot-gajar-halwa10.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
8. Keep on stirring and simmering until the liquids have evaporated and the consistency thickens. The halwa will look glossy and you will some ghee releasing from the sides. Press the cancel button.
![thickened carrot halwa in instant pot](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20487'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F01%2Finstant-pot-gajar-halwa12.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
9. Serve instant pot gajar halwa hot or warm.
![instant pot gajar halwa topped with chopped almonds in a dark blue lined green enamel plate](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20650%20650'%3E%3C/svg%3E)[](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/bookmarklet/?media=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fwp-content%2Fuploads%2F2017%2F01%2Finstant-pot-gajar-halwa2.jpg&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.vegrecipesofindia.com%2Fgajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa%2F&is_video=false&description=Gajar%20Ka%20Halwa%20\(Carrot%20Halwa\) "Save")
### Serving Suggestions
  1. In the cold winters of North India, carrot halwa is best served hot.
  2. You can have gajar halwa chilled if you prefer. It is a personal preference, much like some people prefer pudding hot while others prefer it cold. 
  3. In some restaurants, they serve a combination of this halwa with vanilla ice cream. Believe me: this combination tastes good. It’s like carrot cake pudding a la mode!


### Storage
  * **Refrigerator:** You can store the the halwa in the fridge. It stays well for about 10-12 days, but never lasts that long in our house. When serving from leftovers, just warm the halwa and then serve.
  * **Freezer:** Large quantities of the halwa can be made and frozen. Gajar halwa keeps well in the freezer for about a month.


## Dassana’s Recipe Notes
  * **Buy the best carrots:** It is important to buy tender and juicy carrots so that they are easy to grate. Prepare yourself for an arm workout, unless you have a food processor. Even still, you’ll need to keep on stirring them on regular intervals. Small and medium sized carrots are preferable to larger carrots, which can become woody and tough.
  * **Cooking** : Remember to stir the halwa mixture often when you cook it in a pan. Leaving the skillet or pan unattended will make the milk and carrots burn or brown at the bottom of the pan. So stirring regularly and keeping attention on the halwa while its simmering is a must.
  * **Sweeteners:** While regular sugar is traditional, you can also use unrefined sweeteners like organic raw sugar, jaggery, or even coconut sugar for a deeper flavor. If using jaggery or coconut sugar, add them after the halwa has cooked and cooled slightly to prevent curdling. For low-sugar needs, reduce the overall quantity or consult a health expert before using sugar substitutes.
  * **Grating vs. shredding carrot:** You can either opt to grate or shred the carrots. The carrots can be grated using a hand-held box grater or a food processor. Obviously, a food processor makes grating of the carrots easier, but it is not necessary.
  * **Best pan to make carrot halwa:** I suggest using a heavy iron skillet or kadai (wok) to make the halwa. You can also use any thick-bottomed pan or skillet. 
  * **Scaling:** You can easily halve, double or triple this recipe depending on how many servings you’d like to make.


## **Asked & Answered**
### How many days carrot halwa last?
Store the halwa in the refrigerator for 10 to 12 days. Do not keep it at room temperature as it will get spoiled. You can even freeze the halwa for about a month.
### Can I make this vegan?
Sure! Simply swap in your favorite non-dairy milk and use coconut oil in place of ghee. But note that coconut oil will give its aroma and flavor to the halwa.
### Can I make carrot halwa without nuts?
Absolutely! Feel free to use the toasted seeds of your choice instead, or omit them altogether.
### Can I use red Delhi carrots in summer, or should I wait for winter?
Red Delhi carrots are ideal for halwa due to their sweetness and softness, but they’re seasonal and mostly available in winter. In summer, you can use tender orange or English carrots; just avoid fibrous ones. You may need to slightly increase the sugar, as they’re less sweet than red carrots.

## Gajar Ka Halwa (Traditional Carrot Halwa Recipe)
Author: [Dassana Amit](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/about/)
4.88 from 156 votes
Gajar Ka Halwa is a classic North Indian sweet made by slow-cooking grated carrots in milk, ghee, and sugar, flavored with cardamom and garnished with nuts. My family recipe follows the traditional method and is slow-cooked for rich flavor. This card features the classic version, with three more methods – milkmaid, khoya, and Instant Pot shared in the post.
[Print](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/wprm_print/gajar-ka-halwa-traditional-carrot-halwa-recipe) [Save](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/)[Saved!](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/) [Rate](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#commentform)
Prep Time: 10 minutes minutes
Cook Time: 1 hour hour
Total Time: 1 hour hour 10 minutes minutes
Servings–+
CourseDesserts, Sweets
DietGluten Free, Vegetarian
CuisineNorth Indian, Punjabi
### Ingredients
US CustomaryMetric
  * ▢ 8 to 9 carrots medium, juicy, tender – 650 grams or about 4 to 4.5 cups grated carrots
  * ▢ 4 cups whole milk or full fat organic milk
  * ▢ 4 tablespoons ghee (clarified butter)
  * ▢ 10 to 12 tablespoons sugar  or raw sugar – 180 to 190 grams – add as required
  * ▢ ½ to 1  teaspoon cardamom powder or 5 to 6 green cardamoms powdered finely in a mortar-pestle
  * ▢ 10 to 12 cashews – chopped
  * ▢ 10 to 12 almonds – sliced or chopped
  * ▢ 2 tablespoons golden raisins
  * ▢ 1 pinch saffron strands – optional


### Instructions
#### Preparation
  * First rinse, peel and then grate the carrots (8-9 medium sized tender juicy carrots), either with a hand held grater or in a food processor.
  * You need approx 4 to 4.5 cups grated carrots.


#### Making carrot halwa
  * In a kadai or deep thick bottomed pan combine milk and grated carrots.
  * On a low to medium flame, bring the whole mixture to a boil and then simmer.
  * While the mixture is simmering on a low flame, keep on stirring in between.
  * The grated carrots will cook in the milk and the milk will start to reduce and evaporate.
  * When the milk has 75% reduced, add the ghee, sugar and powdered cardamom to the mixture.
  * Stir well and continue to simmer and cook on a low flame.
  * Do keep on stirring the halwa in between.
  * Towards the end, add the cashews, almonds, saffron and raisins. Simmer the halwa till all the milk is evaporated. Switch off the burner.
  * Serve gajar halwa hot, warm or you can also serve it cold. garnish with some chopped dry fruits while serving.


### Video
### Dassana’s Notes
  * Use fresh, juicy and tender carrots for the best texture and taste.
  * The recipe can be halved or doubled or tripled according to your needs. Note that if you increase the proportions, it will need a lot of simmering and take more time to cook
  * The halwa stays good in the fridge for almost about 10 to 12 days. 
  * Use full-fat milk or whole milk for the best texture and taste.
  * Do not leave the halwa mixture unattended while its simmering and cooking as the ingredients can burn or brown at the bottom of the pan. The carrot halwa mixture has to be stirred often.
  * Add nuts and dry fruits of your choice.
  * This card shows the traditional method. You’ll also find step-by-step instructions for [milkmaid](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-carrot-halwa-with-milkmaid), [khoya](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-gajar-ka-halwa-with-khoya), and [Instant Pot](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#h-instant-pot-carrot-halwa) versions in the post.

  8. **Kirti** says:

Hi Mam, can we replace sugar with jaggery? If yes please let me know if other changes we need to make in recipe when we add jaggery.
[Reply](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#comment-659020)
    1. **[Dassana Amit](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/about)** says:
[Jan 24, 2023 at 10:48 pm](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#comment-659031)
You can replace sugar with jaggery. But with jaggery, it can curdle or split the milk. So I would suggest to add it once the carrot halwa has almost thickened. 
After you add jaggery, it will get a slightly fluid consistency, so simmer for a few minutes more. But remember not to overcook after adding jaggery as the halwa can harden. 
If the consistency is right for you after you add jaggery and it gets dissolved, then you do not need to simmer or cook further. 
You can add the same amount of jaggery as that of sugar mentioned in the recipe. Or add less or more depending on your taste preferences.
[Reply](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#comment-659031)

I’m making the halwa now after trying it in a restaurant and I was wondering, when I grind the cardamom up, do I use only the seeds or the whole pod?
[Reply](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#comment-631729)
    1. **[dassana amit](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/)** says:
[Nov 04, 2017 at 7:44 pm](https://www.vegrecipesofindia.com/gajar-halwa-recipe-gajar-ka-halwa/#comment-631773)
breanna, discard the husks and just use the seeds while grinding. i first add the whole green cardamom pods in the mortar. then crush them lightly. this loosens the outer husk. i remove the husk and either use them in tea or discard them. then powder the cardamom seeds.

[![Image](https://www.bolgen-eiendom.no/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/Web-header-logo.png)](https://www.bolgen-eiendom.no/)
  * [Drømmehytte på Sørlandet](https://www.bolgen-eiendom.no/)
  * [Kontakt](https://www.bolgen-eiendom.no/kontakt/)


[![Image](https://www.bolgen-eiendom.no/wp-content/uploads/2018/11/Web-header-logo-mobile.png)](https://www.bolgen-eiendom.no/)
# Velkommen til din nye drømmehytte på Sørlandet!
## Nyoppført og meget tiltalene hytte med fantastisk utsikt over trysfjorden
Her kan man sikre seg en meget lekker ny og innflyttningsklar hytte på Skarpeid i Søgne. Umiddelbar nærhet til Ålo og Trysnes samt indrefileen av hva Søgne skjærgården kan tilby. Hytten har nydlig utsikt til Trysfjorden fra stue/kjøkken, 1 soverom, terrassen og takterrassen. Meget gode solforhold. Innhold: Gang, bod med egen utgang. Stue med spiseplass og utgang terrasse, åpen kjøkkenløsning med delikat innredning og integrerte hvitevarer. 2 bad med dusjhjørne, baderomsinnredning og vegghengt wc hvorav det ene har direkte inngang fra hovedsoveromet. 3 soverom og gang/TV-stue. Utrolig flott og stor flislagt takterrasse med opplegg for utekjøkken. Mulighet for kjøp eller leie av båtplass i ny båthavn som er under bygging. Denne hytten og beliggenheten må bare oppleves, velkommen til visning!
## Ta kontakt med megler :
Geir Flaa Johansen - Eiendomsmegler MNEF Telefon: [457 70 000](tel:457%2070%20000) Mobil: [950 00 000](tel:950%2000%20000)
[](https://www.finn.no/realestate/leisuresale/ad.html?finnkode=144765617 "Se annonse på FINN.NO")
Beliggenhet: Ved sjøen Boligtype: Hytte Soverom: 3 Primærrom: 90 m² Bruksareal: 98 m² Tomteareal: 405 m² (eiet) Eieform: Eier (Selveier) Byggeår: 2018 Rom: 5 Sengeplasser: 8 
### Fasiliteter
  * Balkong/Terrasse
  * Fiskemulighet
  * Innlagt vann
  * Innlagt strøm
  * Turterreng
  * Offentlig vann/kloakk
  * Utsikt
  * Bilvei frem
  * Båtplass


###### Bølgen Eiendomsutvikling AS
Frode Haaland: [950 73 101](tlf:950%2073%20101)  
Jon Kleiveland: [482 40 910](tlf:482%2040%20910)
### Andre Prosjekter:
## PROSJEKTERT HYTTE MED ALT PÅ EN FLATE OG UTSIKT TIL TRYSFJORDEN
Hytten får følgende innhold: Gang, bod/ vaskerom, bad, 4 soverom, stue og kjøkken. Mulighet for å påvirke materialvalg og innhold hvis man kommer tidlig inn i byggeprosessen. Blant annet er det mulighet for takterrasse og 2 bad.
[](https://www.finn.no/realestate/leisuresale/ad.html?finnkode=163902716 "Se annonse på FINN.NO")
## NY HYTTE I SØGNES SKJÆRGÅRD MED FLOTT TAKTERRASSE
Hytten er delvis ferdig utvendig men ikke innredet. Her er det muligheter for å påvirke innredning og interiør etter dine ønsker. 
[](https://www.finn.no/realestate/leisuresale/ad.html?finnkode=151771787 "Se annonse på FINN.NO")

## Vi feirer 80 år!
Norske skolesekker, ergonomisk formet og elsket siden 1946.
## Anmeldelser
> ★★★★★
> **Flott ryggsekk**
> Rask levering, veldig kul sekk, praktisk og lett, barnet mitt er veldig fornøyd.
> Barbara
> Lübeck, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> **Skolesekk**
> Virkelig en flott skolesekk! Topp kvalitet, veldig lett og flott utseende! rask levering!
> Tamara
> Frohnleiten, Østerrike
> ★★★★★
> **Flott sekk**
> Sitter veldig fint på ryggen. Praktisk med eget rom for vannflaske og matboks. Åpningen gjør det enkelt å sette ting inn og ta dem ut. Flott at det kan utvides. Datteren min elsker det.
> Vigdis
> Drammen, Norge
> ★★★★★
> **Flott skolesekk**
> God kvalitet og virkelig gjennomtenkt. En god mellomløsning mellom den stive bokslignende skolesekken og en høykvalitets ryggsekk. Veldig behagelig og lett. Design er også flott for kule barn.
> Maren
> Heilbronn, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> Alt klart til skolestart
> Superglad førskolegutt har nesten sovet med sekken om natten. Praktisk veske med skrå glidelås og alt tilbehøret du trenger. Anbefalt.
> Erik
> Risør, Norge
> ★★★★★
> **Helt fornøyd**
> Som vanlig er servicen på topp, rask levering og den nye vesken er virkelig deilig og passer SÅ godt på en høy slank jente.
> Britta
> Nexø, Danmark
> ★★★★★
> Jeg er begeistret for Beckmann skolesekkene og tilbehøret som matboksene/drikkeflaskene. Den klassiske skolesekken er perfekt for små barn, på 107 cm. Jeg vil gjerne anbefale det :)
> Daniela
> Bielefeld, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> Veldig fin sekk. Den har alt du trenger til skolehverdagen. Jeg vil anbefale denne sekken fra Beckmann. Hilsen Lydia 10 år
> Hilsen Lydia 10 år
> Oslo, Norge

## Vi bryr oss om vår verden
Bak hver sekk og hvert produkt vi utvikler, ligger en rekke bevisste valg.  
Vi jobber for at hele verdikjeden vår skal være preget av respekt, rettferdighet og omtanke.

## Vi feirer 80 år!
Norske skolesekker, ergonomisk formet og elsket siden 1946.
## Anmeldelser
> ★★★★★
> **Flott ryggsekk**
> Rask levering, veldig kul sekk, praktisk og lett, barnet mitt er veldig fornøyd.
> Barbara
> Lübeck, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> **Skolesekk**
> Virkelig en flott skolesekk! Topp kvalitet, veldig lett og flott utseende! rask levering!
> Tamara
> Frohnleiten, Østerrike
> ★★★★★
> **Flott sekk**
> Sitter veldig fint på ryggen. Praktisk med eget rom for vannflaske og matboks. Åpningen gjør det enkelt å sette ting inn og ta dem ut. Flott at det kan utvides. Datteren min elsker det.
> Vigdis
> Drammen, Norge
> ★★★★★
> **Flott skolesekk**
> God kvalitet og virkelig gjennomtenkt. En god mellomløsning mellom den stive bokslignende skolesekken og en høykvalitets ryggsekk. Veldig behagelig og lett. Design er også flott for kule barn.
> Maren
> Heilbronn, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> Alt klart til skolestart
> Superglad førskolegutt har nesten sovet med sekken om natten. Praktisk veske med skrå glidelås og alt tilbehøret du trenger. Anbefalt.
> Erik
> Risør, Norge
> ★★★★★
> **Helt fornøyd**
> Som vanlig er servicen på topp, rask levering og den nye vesken er virkelig deilig og passer SÅ godt på en høy slank jente.
> Britta
> Nexø, Danmark
> ★★★★★
> Jeg er begeistret for Beckmann skolesekkene og tilbehøret som matboksene/drikkeflaskene. Den klassiske skolesekken er perfekt for små barn, på 107 cm. Jeg vil gjerne anbefale det :)
> Daniela
> Bielefeld, Tyskland
> ★★★★★
> Veldig fin sekk. Den har alt du trenger til skolehverdagen. Jeg vil anbefale denne sekken fra Beckmann. Hilsen Lydia 10 år
> Hilsen Lydia 10 år
> Oslo, Norge

## Vi bryr oss om vår verden
Bak hver sekk og hvert produkt vi utvikler, ligger en rekke bevisste valg.  
Vi jobber for at hele verdikjeden vår skal være preget av respekt, rettferdighet og omtanke.

# Funding for five hydrogen-powered dry-bulk vessels
20/06/2024
[ ![Funding awarded to realise five hydrogen-powered dry-bulk newbuilds](https://workboat365.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/Group-photo_Maris-Fiducia-HAV-Hydrogen-Norwegian-Hydrogen.webp) ](https://workboat365.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/Group-photo_Maris-Fiducia-HAV-Hydrogen-Norwegian-Hydrogen.webp)Foto from left: From the left: Quint Vedder , Maris Fiducia; Jørgen Kopperstad, Norwegian Hydrogen; Richard Inkster, Maris Fiducia Norway; Kristian Osnes, HAV Hydrogen.
**Norwegian state-owned Enova has awarded NOK 300 million in innovation funding to Maris Fiducia Norway AS, with HAV Hydrogen and Norwegian Hydrogen as subcontractors, to develop, build and operate five hydrogen-powered dry-bulk vessels.**
The NOK 300 million grant is awarded under [Enova](https://www.enova.no/)’s “hydrogen in vessels”-programme, which aims to contribute to market change by supporting companies that will use hydrogen as fuel in vessels and support the development of zero emission vessel technologies.
Maris Fiducia Norway, which is the Norwegian operation of Dutch shipowner Maris Fiducia, will manage and operate the five vessels. When built the five dry-bulk carriers will go on hire through a zero-emission time charter agreement with Schulte & Bruns, establishing a commercial operation for vessels operating on hydrogen fuel, proving the feasibility of hydrogen technologies for shipping.
The 6,000 dwt vessels are designed by the Dutch ship designer Ankerbeer with a hull design optimised for efficiency and low fuel consumption. The power plant shall consist of a dual fuel hydrogen ICE combined with fuel cells for auxiliary power.
“We are thrilled that Enova recognised the strong case for the project with this award which is an important milestone for our team in Norway. A little over one year ago we were inspired by a conversation with Jørgen Kopperstad from Norwegian Hydrogen about fuel availability and technical competence in the Norwegian maritime cluster. It has been quite a journey since then, but we are only just starting,” says Markku Vedder, CEO of the Maris Fiducia Group.
In addition to the Dual Fuel ICE, the plan is to equip the five vessels with a Zero Emission Pod – ZEPOD® – a module containing a complete hydrogen energy system for ships, delivered by Norway-based HAV Hydrogen.
Each of the ZEPODs are intended to be delivered with a total capacity of 400 kW, which is enough to run all auxiliary systems on board the bulk carriers. The ZEPODs can be upgraded to 1 MW capacity at a later stage.
HAV Hydrogen is currently developing its first full-scale [ZEPOD,](https://workboat365.com/dnv-has-awarded-hav-group-asa-preliminary-approval-for-its-hydrogen-based-energy-system-2/) which will be ready for testing next year. Preliminary delivery schedule shows that the sequence of delivery will commence from the end of 2025, depending on shipyard.
“This brings us another step closer to the commercial breakthrough for our hydrogen-based energy systems for ships. Everyone knows that the shipping industry needs to lower emissions, but it relies on companies such as Maris Fiducia Norway to pave the way. We are delighted to be part of this partnership and to help develop the future for short sea cargo together with Maris Fiducia and Norwegian Hydrogen,” says Kristian Osnes, managing director of HAV Hydrogen, which is part of Euronext Growth Oslo-listed HAV Group ASA.
**Hydrogen supply**
Leading hydrogen producer and distributor Norwegian Hydrogen will provide security of supply for Maris Fiducia and Schulte & Bruns by providing all the green hydrogen fuel for the five dry bulk carriers.
“We are proud to partner with Maris Fiducia and HAV Hydrogen on this groundbreaking project, where we will ensure the supply of green hydrogen for all the new vessels. This collaboration highlights the practical steps we are taking to decarbonize the maritime sector, and our joint efforts in this partnership will lead to significant emission reductions for Maris Fiducia and Schulte & Bruns. We also look forward to seeing the positive impact these hydrogen-powered vessels will have on advancing green shipping technologies”, says Jens Berge, CEO of Norwegian Hydrogen.
Enova supports tomorrow’s energy and climate technology, so that Norway will reach the climate goals for 2050. Enova manages the Climate and Energy Fund and is a state-owned enterprise owned by the Norwegian Ministry of Climate and Energy.
2,961

[HAV editor](https://www.havgroup.no/hav-group-asa-news/author/hav-editor)19.6.20245 min read
# Funding awarded to realise five hydrogen-powered dry-bulk newbuilds
19 June 2024 – Norwegian state-owned Enova has awarded NOK 300 million in innovation funding to Maris Fiducia Norway AS, with HAV Hydrogen and Norwegian Hydrogen as subcontractors, to develop, build and operate five hydrogen-powered dry-bulk vessels.
The NOK 300 million grant is awarded under Enova’s “hydrogen in vessels”-programme, which aims to contribute to market change by supporting companies that will use hydrogen as fuel in vessels and support the development of zero emission vessel technologies. 
Maris Fiducia Norway, which is the Norwegian operation of Dutch shipowner Maris Fiducia, will manage and operate the five vessels. When built the five dry-bulk carriers will go on hire through a zero-emission time charter agreement with Schulte & Bruns, establishing a commercial operation for vessels operating on hydrogen fuel, proving the feasibility of hydrogen technologies for shipping.
The 6,000 dwt vessels are designed by the Dutch ship designer Ankerbeer with a hull design optimised for efficiency and low fuel consumption. The power plant shall consist of a dual fuel hydrogen ICE combined with fuel cells for auxiliary power. 
“We are thrilled that Enova recognised the strong case for the project with this award which is an important milestone for our team in Norway. A little over one year ago we were inspired by a conversation with Jørgen Kopperstad from Norwegian Hydrogen about fuel availability and technical competence in the Norwegian maritime cluster. It has been quite a journey since then, but we are only just starting,” says Markku Vedder, CEO of the Maris Fiducia Group.
![S_Sulafjorden_Maris Fiducia Norway AS_HAV Hydrogen_Norwegian Hydrogen](https://www.havgroup.no/hs-fs/hubfs/S_Sulafjorden_Maris%20Fiducia%20Norway%20AS_HAV%20Hydrogen_Norwegian%20Hydrogen.jpg?width=2200&height=837&name=S_Sulafjorden_Maris%20Fiducia%20Norway%20AS_HAV%20Hydrogen_Norwegian%20Hydrogen.jpg)
**H2 system on board**
In addition to the Dual Fuel ICE, the plan is to equip the five vessels with a Zero Emission Pod – ZEPOD® – a module containing a complete hydrogen energy system for ships, delivered by Norway-based HAV Hydrogen. 
Each of the ZEPODs are intended to be delivered with a total capacity of 400 kW, which is enough to run all auxiliary systems on board the bulk carriers. The ZEPODs can be upgraded to 1 MW capacity at a later stage. 
HAV Hydrogen is currently developing its first full-scale ZEPOD, which will be ready for testing next year. Preliminary delivery schedule shows that the sequence of delivery will commence from the end of 2025, depending on shipyard. 
“This brings us another step closer to the commercial breakthrough for our hydrogen-based energy systems for ships. Everyone knows that the shipping industry needs to lower emissions, but it relies on companies such as Maris Fiducia Norway to pave the way. We are delighted to be part of this partnership and to help develop the future for short sea cargo together with Maris Fiducia and Norwegian Hydrogen,” says Kristian Osnes, managing director of HAV Hydrogen, which is part of Euronext Growth Oslo-listed HAV Group ASA.
**Hydrogen supply**
Leading hydrogen producer and distributor Norwegian Hydrogen will provide security of supply for Maris Fiducia and Schulte & Bruns by providing all the green hydrogen fuel for the five dry bulk carriers. 
“We are proud to partner with Maris Fiducia and HAV Hydrogen on this groundbreaking project, where we will ensure the supply of green hydrogen for all the new vessels. This collaboration highlights the practical steps we are taking to decarbonize the maritime sector, and our joint efforts in this partnership will lead to significant emission reductions for Maris Fiducia and Schulte & Bruns. We also look forward to seeing the positive impact these hydrogen-powered vessels will have on advancing green shipping technologies”, says Jens Berge, CEO of Norwegian Hydrogen. 
Enova supports tomorrow's energy and climate technology, so that Norway will reach the climate goals for 2050. Enova manages the Climate and Energy Fund and is a state-owned enterprise owned by the Norwegian Ministry of Climate and Energy. 
(ENDS)  
  

![Group photo_Maris Fiducia-HAV Hydrogen-Norwegian Hydrogen](https://www.havgroup.no/hs-fs/hubfs/Group%20photo_Maris%20Fiducia-HAV%20Hydrogen-Norwegian%20Hydrogen.png?width=966&height=1080&name=Group%20photo_Maris%20Fiducia-HAV%20Hydrogen-Norwegian%20Hydrogen.png)
_Foto from left: From the left: Quint Vedder , Maris Fiducia; Jørgen Kopperstad, Norwegian Hydrogen; Richard Inkster, Maris Fiducia Norway; Kristian Osnes, HAV Hydrogen._
**For further information, please contact:**
Quint Vedder, Head of Innovations & Projects, Maris Fiducia  
Quint.vedder@maris-fiducia.com | +31 651 132 560
Kristian Osnes, Managing Director, HAV Hydrogen  
Kristian.osnes@havhydrogen.no | +47 996 29 165
Marielle Furnes Mannseth, Group Brand & Sustainability Officer Norwegian Hydrogen AS   
marielle@nh2.no | +47 93 40 21 40**  
  
**
**About Maris Fiducia  
** Maris Fiducia, a family-owned company, was established in 2007 and aims to be an innovative leader and best-in-class supplier of environmentally friendly maritime transport solutions. Through its subsidiaries, the group operates a quality fleet of well-maintained tankers and dry cargo ships which are in daily operation. The group has a clear strategy towards environmentally friendly sea transport and logistics solutions for well-known customers, and transport of sustainable products to different industry sectors. The shipping company operates primarily in the North Sea basin, the Baltics and SECA areas (Sulphur Emission Control Area), but also has missions over longer distances and intercontinentally. In addition to Maris Fiducia Norway, the other group companies Grona Ship Management, South End Tanker Management (Wet Cargo) and Q-Shipping (Dry Cargo) support daily operations and the newbuilding programs. 
**About HAV Hydrogen |[www.havhydrogen.no](https://www.havhydrogen.no)  
**HAV Hydrogen delivers complete and scalable hydrogen systems for use on board large and small vessels, newbuilds and retrofitted. Based on our Norwegian heritage, experience, quality focus and innovative solutions, our insight provides our customers with a head start, increases their competitiveness, and enables them to realize the green shift towards a sustainable future at sea.**  
**
**About Norwegian Hydrogen  
** Norwegian Hydrogen AS builds and operates a comprehensive network of production sites and distribution systems for green hydrogen throughout the Nordics, tailored to meet the future requirements for zero-emission energy in a wide range of mobility and industrial segments. The company is backed by several strong industrial owners with a significant footprint within the global hydrogen value chain, such as Flakk Group, Hexagon Purus, Fortescue, Mitsui & Co., Ltd, and Norwegian hydropower company Tafjord. Norwegian Hydrogen’s head office is in Ålesund, Norway, and they also have offices in Oslo, Helsinki, Copenhagen and Stockholm.

[](https://nauticalvoice.com/)_/_[Decarbonization](https://nauticalvoice.com/topics/decarbonization/) _/_ Maris Fiducia team up with HAV Hydrogen, Norwegian Hydrogen and Ankerbeer for zero emission bulk shipping
#  Maris Fiducia team up with HAV Hydrogen, Norwegian Hydrogen and Ankerbeer for zero emission bulk shipping 
## Maris Fiducia Norway Pioneering Hydrogen-Powered Vessels in Europe 
[NV Tech Desk](https://nauticalvoice.com/author/nv-tech-desk/ "NV Tech Desk")May 14, 2024Last Updated: May 14, 2024
![HAV Hydrogen](https://nauticalvoice.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/images-2.png)
Maris Fiducia Norway has entered into cooperation agreements with maritime technology company HAV Hydrogen and the hydrogen producer and distributor Norwegian Hydrogen with the objective of developing, building, and operating hydrogen-powered dry-bulk vessels in Europe.
Upon successful realization of the project, the vessels will go on hire through a zero-emission time charter agreement with Schulte & Bruns, establishing a commercial operation for vessels operating on hydrogen fuel and proving the feasibility of hydrogen technologies for shipping.
“Maris Fiducia is dedicated to shaping the future of maritime transportation towards environmental sustainability, and our chartering partners Schulte & Bruns share this vision. Using hydrogen as fuel is a natural step in this development, and the versatility of HAV Hydrogen’s ZEPOD, coupled with Norwegian Hydrogen’s hydrogen infrastructure, represents what is required to realise such ambitions,” says Markku Vedder, CEO of the Maris Fiducia Group.
The group operates a fleet of over twenty dry cargo and tanker vessels, and its Norwegian subsidiary, Maris Fiducia Norway AS, shall operate the hydrogen-powered vessels. The group’s primary focus is environmentally friendly maritime transportation logistics for reputable customers. The company has decades of experience in the development of advanced ship designs and solutions for sustainable shipping, with a track-record of over 100 new builds.
![](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20600%20338'%3E%3C/svg%3E)
HAV Hydrogen brings their specialist competence in the development of complete and scalable hydrogen-based energy systems to the partnership. The company has developed and obtained approval in principle from DNV for its Zero Emission Pod (ZEPOD®), a deckhouse containing a complete hydrogen energy system for ships. Installed power from a ZEPOD® can be used for main propulsion systems or for auxiliary power onboard a vessel. Subject to potential innovation funding, Maris Fiducia’s chosen shipyard will order ZEPODs from HAV Hydrogen for use on board the vessels.
“As a specialist supplier and integrator of maritime fuel cell solutions, there is obviously a limit to how much we can influence the maritime industry on our own. By collaborating with a shipowner and a producer of green hydrogen, we enable a value chain approach that can tear down further barriers towards the realization of hydrogen as maritime fuel,” says Kristian Osnes, managing director of HAV Hydrogen, which is part of Euronext Growth Oslo-listed HAV Group ASA.
Norwegian Hydrogen is building an array of hydrogen production sites throughout the Nordics and will operate a comprehensive distribution network for green hydrogen. This capability shall provide security of supply for Maris Fiducia and Schulte & Bruns.
“The cooperation with Maris Fiducia and their founder, Markku Vedder, proves there are shipowners with the vision and capability to develop zero-emission shipping solutions. We look forward to the journey onwards together with Maris Fiducia, establishing corridors for sustainable shipping throughout Europe”, says Jens Berge, CEO of Norwegian Hydrogen.
[Source link ](https://hydrogeneurope.eu/maris-fiducia-teams-up-with-hav-hydrogen-norwegian-hydrogen-and-ankerbeer-for-zero-emission-bulk-shipping/)
